name: Hash Set Sequence Starts
summary: Put every value in a set and only walk a run from its start (the value with no predecessor).
visualizationIntent: init: the values are shown as a membership set, longest = 0; loop(num): the candidate num is marked special; check(start?): the readout asks whether num-1 is present â€” only a value with no predecessor is a run start; update: from a start, current begins at num; loop(while): the probe current+1 is marked compared and, if present, the run extends â€” the whole run from start to head lights as path with the head current; move: current climbs; update(longest): best length refreshed; return: LONGEST RUN holds the answer, and the learner sees each run was walked only from its start.

lineExplanations.3: Put every value into a set so membership checks are O(1).
lineExplanations.4: Track the longest run length found so far.
lineExplanations.5: Consider each distinct value in the set.
lineExplanations.6: A value starts a run only if its predecessor (num - 1) is absent.
lineExplanations.7: Begin walking upward from this start value.
lineExplanations.8: The run has length 1 so far.
lineExplanations.9: While the next consecutive value is in the set...
lineExplanations.10: Step up to that next value.
lineExplanations.11: The run has grown by one.
lineExplanations.12: Keep the best run length seen so far.
lineExplanations.13: Return the length of the longest consecutive run.

syntaxExplanations.1: class Solution: the wrapper class the platform runs.
syntaxExplanations.2: def longestConsecutive(self, nums): the method receiving the numbers.
syntaxExplanations.3: set(nums) builds a hash set of the distinct values.
syntaxExplanations.5: for num in numbers iterates over each distinct value.
syntaxExplanations.6: num - 1 not in numbers is True when nothing precedes num.
syntaxExplanations.7: current = num copies the start value into a walker.
syntaxExplanations.9: current + 1 in numbers tests membership in O(1).
syntaxExplanations.11: current_length += 1 counts the newly reached value.
syntaxExplanations.12: max(longest, current_length) keeps whichever is larger.
syntaxExplanations.13: return longest hands back the answer.
